author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Microsoft significantly increases Xbox prices worldwide and withdraws 2TB model citing "components crisis"

Microsoft has announced that it is increasing the price of Xbox worldwide from August 2, blaming increased costs for console storage and memory. The 512 GB models will increase by $100 and the 1TB model will increase by $150; the 2TB variant will be withdrawn. The company did not immediately confirm pricing for non-US markets.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
"Mission Impossible is high on my list" – how Paramount plans to use co-dev to exploit its archive, while building its own all-new IP

Just before Summer Games Fest kicked off, Paramount Skydance announced it was merging its video games divisions into a new entity called Paramount Games Studio.

text:
VC fund Vgames launches new $10m Indie Fund

Venture capital firm Vgames is launching a new $10 million Indie Fund.

text:
Circana: Nintendo Switch 2 helped US physical game spending increase year-on-year for first time since 2009

The Nintendo Switch 2 helped US physical games spending see its first year-on-year increase since 2009.

text:
Tencent, Xbox, Game Angels, Ansible and Flick to speak on Game Republic New Horizons investor stage

The organisers of the Game Republic New Horizons conference have announced the first speakers to take to the stage in its Investor Zone.

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Micron says chip shortage will "improve gradually" in 2028

The CEO of chip giant Micron, Sanjay Mehrotra, has said that the AI-fueled global RAM and NAND shortage will continue through 2027 and "improve gradually" the following year.

Read more

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
FromSoftware parent CEO keeps position following activist investors' disappointment in firm's handling of Elden Ring

The CEO of FromSoftware parent company Kadokawa Corporation, Takeshi Natsuno, has retained his position after an activist investor group tried to oust him.

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Sony is reportedly enforcing "stricter guidelines" against so-called "shovelware" PlayStation games

Sony is seemingly enforcing "stricter guidelines" by cutting ties with alleged "shovelware" publishers like the Brazilian-based Afil Games.

author: GamesIndustry.biz
text:
Jagex confirms "landmark publishing partnerships" to bring RuneScape to Japan, South Korea, and South East Asia

RuneScape developer Jagex has announced two publishing partnerships to bring RuneScape: Dragonwilds to Japan, South Korea, and South East Asia.
